title: Guide to Using the Site Tab on the SiteFlow Mobile App
metaTitle: Guide to Using the Site Tab on the SiteFlow Mobile App
metaDescription: Use the Site tab on the SiteFlow mobile app to view daily site activity, raise material requests, check site photos, and create and download the Daily Progress Report.
author: SiteFlow Operations Team
body:
Guide to using the Site tab in a project on the SiteFlow mobile app — reading the daily site summary, checking site photos, viewing ongoing tasks, raising and viewing material requests, and creating and downloading the Daily Progress Report (DPR).

Prerequisites

  • You must be inside a project

Opening the Site Tab

Open a project and tap the Site tab.

Top Bar

The top of the Site tab shows three items:

Use the left and right arrows (date navigator) to move between days and see the summary for any date.

Day Summary Tiles

The main section shows a snapshot of what was recorded on the selected day.

A 0 on any tile means nothing was recorded for that category on that day. Use this as a quick daily check — if Equipment shows 0 and machines were on site, the equipment log may be missing.

Tap the photo icon on any tile to add photos tagged to that category (e.g. tap the photo icon on Material Received to attach a delivery photo to that day’s GRN activity).

Site Photos

The Site Photos row shows a thumbnail strip of all photos uploaded for the selected day.

  • Tap View All to see the full photo gallery for that day
  • Tap the camera icon on the left to add a new photo for the selected date

Photos uploaded here are tagged to the project and date and appear in the DPR’s Site Photos section.

Ongoing Tasks

The Ongoing Tasks section lists tasks currently in progress, showing the task name and current progress (e.g. “Fan Installation — 85 nos completed”).

Tap View All to open the full task list for the project.

Material Requests

Tap the Material Requests button at the top of the Site tab to open the Material module.

Four sub-tabs appear:

The Material Requests shortcut on the Site tab means the supervisor does not need to navigate to a separate Material tab to raise a request — it is accessible directly from here.

Creating and Downloading a DPR

The DPR (Daily Progress Report) is a PDF summary of the project’s activity for a selected date. It compiles data already entered in the app — attendance, materials, equipment, subcontractor progress, and site photos — into a single shareable report.

Steps

  1. From the Site tab, scroll to the bottom and tap Create DPR
  2. A section selector appears. All sections are checked by default.
  3. Uncheck any sections you do not want to include in this DPR
  4. Tap Download PDF
  5. A preview of the DPR opens — tap Download to save it to your device

DPR Sections

Sharing the DPR

After downloading, tap the share icon to send the PDF directly to the project WhatsApp group or any other contact. Sharing the DPR with clients or management at the end of each working day keeps all stakeholders updated without requiring them to log into SiteFlow.

Tips

  • The day summary tiles are the fastest daily check. A 0 on any tile signals a missing entry. If Material Received shows 0 but materials arrived that day, a GRN has not been created.
  • Use the date navigator to go back and review previous days. If a report is being prepared for yesterday or last week, navigate to the correct date before creating the DPR.
  • Uncheck sections in the DPR selector when only specific data is needed. A client update DPR can include just Work Summary and Site Photos, skipping Labour and Material details.
  • The AI Recommendations section in the DPR highlights critical items automatically — tasks falling behind schedule, inventory running low, or pending material requests. Review this section before the daily team briefing.
  • The photo icon on each day summary tile lets you attach photos to a specific activity category. Tagging a photo to Material Received connects it to that day’s GRN activity for the DPR.

If Something Goes Wrong
